metadata.description: IFoA CS1 Actuarial Statistics syllabus for the 2026 examinations.

topics.title: Random Variables and Distributions
topics.description: Understand and apply key concepts related to random variables, including probability functions, distribution functions, expectation, variance, and higher moments.

learning_outcomes.description: Define and distinguish between discrete, continuous and mixed random variables.
Calculate and interpret expectation, variance, skewness and kurtosis.
Derive the moment generating function and cumulant generating function for common distributions.

topics.title: Common Statistical Distributions
topics.description: Apply the binomial, Poisson, negative binomial, exponential, gamma, normal, log-normal, chi-squared, t and F distributions.

learning_outcomes.description: Derive moments, mode and quantiles for each distribution.
Identify appropriate distributions for real-world modelling scenarios.

topics.title: Generating Functions and Sums of Random Variables
topics.description: Use probability generating functions, moment generating functions and cumulant generating functions to derive properties of sums of independent random variables, including the central limit theorem.

learning_outcomes.description: Derive exact distributions of sums of independent random variables using generating functions.
State and apply the central limit theorem for a single sample and for two independent samples.

topics.title: Joint Distributions
topics.description: Understand joint (bivariate) distributions, including joint probability/density functions, marginal distributions, conditional distributions, independence, covariance, correlation and expectation of functions of two random variables.

learning_outcomes.description: Calculate marginal and conditional distributions from a joint distribution.
Determine whether two random variables are independent and calculate covariance and correlation.

topics.title: Bayesian Statistics
topics.description: Understand and apply Bayesian methods, including the prior distribution, posterior distribution, conjugate priors, loss functions and Bayesian estimators.

learning_outcomes.description: Derive posterior distributions using Bayes' theorem for common conjugate families.
Calculate Bayesian estimators under quadratic and absolute error loss.

topics.title: Sampling and Statistical Inference
topics.description: Apply methods of point estimation including the method of moments and maximum likelihood estimation. Construct confidence intervals and perform hypothesis tests, including likelihood ratio tests.

learning_outcomes.description: Derive maximum likelihood estimators and evaluate their properties.
Construct confidence intervals for means, proportions and variances.
